title: salix cinerascens
intro: Salix cinerascens, also known as the willow cinerea, is a type of shrub or small tree belonging to the willow family (Salicaceae). Traditionally, Salix cinerascens has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits associated with Salix cinerascens include its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce fever. Salix cinerascens is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
